mnemonics.title: Ethernet Frame Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Pretty Sweet Data Fills Complete Bytes: Preamble(7), SFD(1), Source(6), Destination(6), Type(2), Data(46-1500), FCS(4)

mnemonics.title: CSMA/CD vs CSMA/CA
mnemonics.content: Collision Detection is for Wired (Ethernet) - Detect and stop; Collision Avoidance is for Wireless - Avoid before it happens

mnemonics.title: MAC Address Format
mnemonics.content: Manufacturers Own Unique Identification (OUI) + Network Interface Controller ID

acronyms.usage: Sublayer controlling access to shared medium; also refers to MAC addresses (hardware addresses)

acronyms.expansion: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection
acronyms.usage: MAC protocol used in wired Ethernet - detects collisions and stops transmission

acronyms.expansion: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Avoidance
acronyms.usage: MAC protocol used in wireless networks - avoids collisions before they happen
Error detection field in Ethernet frame using CRC-32
First 24 bits of MAC address identifying the manufacturer
Marks the beginning of the Ethernet frame (10101011)

acronyms.expansion: Request to Send / Clear to Send
acronyms.usage: Handshake mechanism in CSMA/CA to avoid hidden node problem

visualTips.visualization: Think of a letter: Preamble = address stamp, SFD = 'Dear' start, Source/Destination = sender/receiver addresses, Type = what language (protocol), Data = the letter content, FCS = wax seal (tamper detection)
Imagine people in a room (shared medium): Listen before speaking (carrier sense), if two speak at once - both stop and wait random times (collision detection + backoff)
Slot machine reels - first collision: 0-1 wait, second: 0-3, third: 0-7... doubling each time, maxing at 0-1023 slots

analogies.analogy: A conference room where multiple people want to speak at once - without rules, chaos ensues. MAC protocols are the rules for who speaks when.
Walkie-talkie protocol - 'Is channel clear?' If yes, transmit. If you hear someone else, say 'Collision!' and both wait random times.
Asking permission before speaking at a meeting - 'May I speak?' Wait for 'Yes' before talking, reducing chances of talking over someone.
Like a fingerprint or serial number - unique to each device, permanently assigned, identifies the manufacturer and specific unit.
Like a physical mail envelope: Preamble = packaging, Addresses = 'To' and 'From', Type = letter category, Data = the letter itself, FCS = tamper-evident seal.
